nodes.name: AI Check Email

parameters.text:
=Classify the email with decimal values (0 to 1) for isUnwantedConfidence, isMarketingConfidence, and isSpamConfidence, where 0 means clearly wanted (e.g., billing, invoices, orders, job applications, security) and 1 means clearly unwanted (e.g., promotions, setup reminders, irrelevant alerts); treat system-generated alerts or device activity (like sound played, device found, location pinged) as unwanted unless they are security-related; use 0.5 as the baseline for deletion and provide a concise briefReason explaining the classification.

{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.notes: This outputParserStructured node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: If Unwanted Marketing or Spam
nodes.notes: This if node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Telegram Sent Email Deleted Notification

parameters.text: =Email Deleted | {{ $('If Unwanted Marketing or Spam').item.json.output.emailFrom }} | {{ $('If Unwanted Marketing or Spam').item.json.output.briefReason }}

nodes.notes: This telegram node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Telegram Sent Email Not Deleted Notification

parameters.text: =Skipping Email | {{ $('If Unwanted Marketing or Spam').item.json.output.emailFrom }} | {{ $('If Unwanted Marketing or Spam').item.json.output.briefReason }}

nodes.notes: This telegram node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Telegram Sent AI Error Notification

parameters.text: =AI Error | Can't Check Email | Error: {{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

nodes.notes: This telegram node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

assignments.name: page

originalWorkflowJson.name: Gmail Workflow
originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Gmail Workflow. This workflow processes data and performs automated tasks.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Gmail Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.text:
=Classify the email with decimal values (0 to 1) for isUnwantedConfidence, isMarketingConfidence, and isSpamConfidence, where 0 means clearly wanted (e.g., billing, invoices, orders, job applications, security) and 1 means clearly unwanted (e.g., promotions, setup reminders, irrelevant alerts); treat system-generated alerts or device activity (like sound played, device found, location pinged) as unwanted unless they are security-related; use 0.5 as the baseline for deletion and provide a concise briefReason explaining the classification.

{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

assignments.name: page
assignments.value: ={{ ($('Forward Prev Page Num').isExecuted) ? $('Forward Prev Page Num').first().json.prevPage + 1 : $('Initialize Loop Vars').first().json.page }}

parameters.text: =AI Error | Can't Check Email | Error: {{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

nodes.name: Telegram Sent Delete Email Failed Notification

parameters.text: =Email Deleted | {{ $('If Unwanted Marketing or Spam').item.json.output.emailFrom }} | {{ $('If Unwanted Marketing or Spam').item.json.output.briefReason }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Palm  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Gmail Get Email" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "GmailDeleteEmail" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Check Email" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Unwanted Email Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Unwanted Marketing or Spam" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram Sent Email Deleted Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram Sent Email Not Deleted Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram Sent AI Error Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram Sent Delete Email Failed Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Success" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Aggregate" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Increment Loop Var".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Forward Prev Page Num".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Initialize Loop Vars".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
